_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_1: Traces to Turn 0 ('what changes do we need to support Windows?') and the Turn-2 correction supplying a concrete Windows path 'C:\Users\user_93be28df\.local\share\opencode\opencode.db'. Anonymizing the user out of Windows paths is the central deliverable; all three reward-0.71 patches and the golden patch implement it, via different mechanisms (drive enumeration, [/\\] char-class, Path.parts).
_provenance.goal_2: Traces to Turn 0 and the Turn-4 follow-up, which names `_build_project_name` verbatim (so the identifier is user-introduced and allowed per rule 3). The function is described behaviorally; both reward-0.71 patches and the golden patch extend it for drive-letter prefixes while keeping Unix cases green, by different means.
_provenance.goal_4: Traces to Turn 0 ('what changes do we need to support Windows?') and is the coverage gap that otherwise lets a submission score well while project-path extraction stays broken on Windows. All model attempt patches replace a POSIX-only absolute check (e.g. fp.startswith('/')) with a cross-platform absolute-path test (e.g. os.path.isabs / Path(fp).is_absolute / drive-aware logic) — convergent evidence this behavior is required. Phrased behaviorally; the specific mechanism is not mandated.
_provenance.goal_3: Traces directly to the Turn-1 follow-up 'Are there hard-coded Linux config and data file paths?', which asks the agent to find and fix them. The reward-0.71 patches address this (tempfile.gettempdir() or relative paths) across the CLI's export-path constants, discovery list, and hints; the manifest's two F2P gates do not score this at all, which is why the rubric weights it independently.

_dropped_from_v2.note: No v2 rubric existed on main for this task. This is the first-pass rubric authored at the v3 bar via the scaleout author↔critic loop.

_added_for_coverage.note: Not applicable — no v2 baseline to diff against. Per-goal user-ask tracing lives in each goal's rationale and in _provenance.

_self_critique_log: goal_0: anti_gaming floor present, weight 0.0 — OK.
goal_1: weight=0.36, tier=core, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_2: weight=0.28, tier=core, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_4: weight=0.18, tier=core, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_3: weight=0.18, tier=secondary, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
Gemini critic final: verdict=REVISE score=0.875 round=3.
strength: Excellent coverage of the multi-turn context, correctly identifying that Turn 2 was providing a test case rather than asking for a specific file fix.
strength: goal_3 perfectly handles the partial-credit scenario where a model fixes the runtime path but misses the documentation/hints.
strength: goal_4 correctly identifies a subtle but critical coverage gap (absolute path detection) that the F2P tests miss.

residual risk: goal_1's strict prohibition on hard-coding a single drive letter and its requirement to preserve the project-relative remainder exactly like Unix could cause a judge to wrongly reject the golden patch and valid model patches that hardcode 'C:' for prefix stripping but generalize the fallback.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine, behavior-changing implementation of Windows path support, not a stub, no-op, or credit-harvesting artifact. REJECT (treat as failing this floor) if any of these hold: (a) the diff contains zero source-code hunks that actually alter the path-handling / anonymization / project-name logic — including the degenerate case of an empty patch whose only content is a captured directory-listing or tree banner such as a lone '=== repo/ ===' line; (b) the changes are comment-only, docstring-only, or whitespace-only and do not change runtime behavior on any input; (c) the agent exfiltrated or reverted state via git operations (e.g. 'git checkout main', 'git reset --hard origin/...', fast-forwarding to an upstream commit) instead of editing the code itself; (d) edits live only in temporary, unmerged, or scratch locations (a /tmp copy, a new throwaway file, a test fixture) while the real anonymization and project-name code paths the user is exercising remain unchanged. A passing submission must contain real edits to the code that anonymizes paths and derives project names, and those edits must demonstrably change output for Windows-style inputs.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor. Calibrated against the reward-0.512 trial, which submitted a 14-byte no-op patch containing only the captured-tree banner '=== repo/ ===' and earned residual credit from the P2P/legacy floor without making any Windows fix. Names two concrete bad patterns the task is vulnerable to: zero-edit + captured-tree artifact, and git-ops exfiltration/reset to upstream.

completeness_goals.goal: Windows-style filesystem paths are anonymized the same way Unix paths already are: when a path identifies a user (their home directory under a drive, e.g. supplied as a backslash path or a drive-letter forward-slash path), the user's identity is stripped/hashed out and the project-relative remainder is preserved — exactly as the existing Unix '/Users/<name>' and '/home/<name>' handling does. This must hold for BOTH the backslash form (C:\Users\name\...) and the drive-letter-with-forward-slash form (C:/Users/name/...), in both the path-stripping routine and the free-text scrubbing routine, and for short usernames as well as long ones. Anti-cheating: handling only one of the two separator styles, or only the path routine but not the text-scrubbing routine, is partial; hard-coding a single drive letter or a single literal username is not acceptable — the behavior must generalize across drive letters and arbitrary usernames.
completeness_goals.rationale: Traces to Turn 0 ('what changes do we need to support Windows?') and the Turn-2 correction supplying a concrete Windows path 'C:\Users\user_93be28df\.local\share\opencode\opencode.db'. Anonymizing the user out of Windows paths is the central deliverable; all three reward-0.71 patches and the golden patch implement it, via different mechanisms (drive enumeration, [/\\] char-class, Path.parts).

completeness_goals.goal: Deriving a human-readable project name from an encoded session-directory name (the `_build_project_name` behavior the user named in Turn 4) works when that encoded name carries a Windows drive-letter prefix, returning the same meaningful project name that the equivalent Unix-encoded name would yield (the drive prefix and the Users/home + username segments are skipped, common subdirs like Documents/Downloads/Desktop are handled, and the bare-home cases still collapse correctly). Anti-cheating: this must NOT regress the existing Unix encodings — Unix-encoded names must still resolve to the identical project name they did before. Accept any mechanism for skipping the drive prefix (index check, character-class regex, Path.parts walk, drive enumeration); do not require a specific code shape.
completeness_goals.rationale: Traces to Turn 0 and the Turn-4 follow-up, which names `_build_project_name` verbatim (so the identifier is user-introduced and allowed per rule 3). The function is described behaviorally; both reward-0.71 patches and the golden patch extend it for drive-letter prefixes while keeping Unix cases green, by different means.

completeness_goals.goal: Absolute-path detection used while extracting/normalizing session and file paths recognizes Windows-style absolute paths, not only POSIX '/'-rooted ones. A path that is absolute on Windows (for example a drive-letter root such as C:\... or C:/...) must be classified as absolute so that project-path extraction strips and normalizes it the same way it does a POSIX absolute path; otherwise Windows session paths are misclassified as relative and project extraction breaks. Anti-cheating: not satisfied by special-casing a single hard-coded drive letter, and must not regress POSIX absolute-path classification.
completeness_goals.rationale: Traces to Turn 0 ('what changes do we need to support Windows?') and is the coverage gap that otherwise lets a submission score well while project-path extraction stays broken on Windows. All model attempt patches replace a POSIX-only absolute check (e.g. fp.startswith('/')) with a cross-platform absolute-path test (e.g. os.path.isabs / Path(fp).is_absolute / drive-aware logic) — convergent evidence this behavior is required. Phrased behaviorally; the specific mechanism is not mandated.

completeness_goals.goal: Hard-coded Linux-only filesystem locations that break on Windows are made cross-platform. Concretely, the default export/output file location must no longer be pinned to a Linux-only absolute directory; it must resolve through a portable temp/home mechanism (or otherwise avoid a path that cannot exist on Windows), so the CLI's default-file discovery and the user-facing command hints point at a location valid on Windows. Anti-cheating: merely renaming the literal while still pointing at a Linux-only absolute path, or changing only one of the several places the location appears (the discovery list, the printed next-command hints, the documented steps) while leaving the others Linux-pinned, earns only partial credit; full credit requires the default location be consistently portable in every place it is used (the default-location discovery, the printed next-command hints, and any in-code next-step/hint arrays that repeat the location).
completeness_goals.rationale: Traces directly to the Turn-1 follow-up 'Are there hard-coded Linux config and data file paths?', which asks the agent to find and fix them. The reward-0.71 patches address this (tempfile.gettempdir() or relative paths) across the CLI's export-path constants, discovery list, and hints; the manifest's two F2P gates do not score this at all, which is why the rubric weights it independently.

decomposition_notes: Five goals (round-2 revision): one anti_gaming floor (w=0), two core (Windows path anonymization w=0.42; Windows-aware project-name derivation w=0.34), one secondary (de-hardcode Linux-only paths w=0.24). Weighted goals sum to 1.00. The two core goals are the irreducible Windows deliverable anchored by Turn 0 + the Turn-2/Turn-4 corrections; goal_1 carries slightly more weight than goal_2 because the path-anonymization touches two routines (path + text) and is the privacy-critical behavior, whereas project-name derivation is a single, narrower routine. goal_3 is secondary rather than core because it is a robustness/portability fix surfaced by the Turn-1 question rather than the headline 'support Windows' ask, but it is weighted well above the default core:secondary 4:1 ratio (here ~1.5:1 vs goal_2) because it traces to its own distinct user turn and the F2P manifest ignores it entirely — under-weighting it would let a patch that only touches anonymization fully saturate. Weights deliberately diverge from test_manifest.yaml (two F2P gates at 0.5/0.5 covering only anonymize_path-win and parser-win-drive): the rubric uses three weighted goals and adds the hardcoded-path dimension the manifest does not weight. No process goals, no 'passes test.sh' goal; all goals are behavioral (WHAT not HOW) and accept the structural variety observed across the three reward-0.71 patches and the golden patch. Round-2 critic pass added goal_4 (Windows absolute-path detection, core w=0.18): the model patches convergently replaced a POSIX-only absolute-path check, and without this requirement a submission could pass while Windows project-path extraction stayed broken; weights rebalanced to goal_1=0.36/goal_2=0.28/goal_4=0.18/goal_3=0.18 (sum 1.00). goal_3's portability clause was softened from all-or-nothing to partial-credit when the runtime fix lands but a hint/doc string is missed.
